rulings.Case Title: FANX, INC. VS. JAMES DAVIS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 12, 2024 line 3. PLAINTIFF FANX, INC. , A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Summary Adjudication is DENIED. FANX, INC motion is denied as the moving party failed to shift its burden. The Court has already ruled that the tort claims are not to proceed in arbitration. Additionally, Andrew Won's motion is denied as this motion was filed while Mr. Won is in default. The Court notes that Mr. Won, a prevailing party on the Motion to Vacate Default failed to comply with the Court's September 24, 2024 minute ordered ordering him to prepare a proposed order in accordance with CRC 3.1312(b). rulings.Case Title: NAM SHI LEE VS. EUGENE JUN KWOCK TOM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 12, 2024 line 8. PLAINTIFF NAM LEE , AKA NANCY LEE, AS TRUSTEE OF THE LEE REVOCABLE TRUST U/A/D MARCH 25 MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden as to the motion for summary judgment. No competent admissible evidence that Defendants are in possession. Motion for summary adjudication is denied. Plaintiff failed to provide any evidence that (1) there is no landlord-tenant relationship between the parties; (2) that Defendants possess no evidence to support their affirmative defenses, e.g. that Plaintiff propounded relevant discovery and Defendants provided factually devoid responses. The Court further notes the inconsistency in Plaintiff's filings: on the one hand Plaintiff lists in the caption of this complaint that this action is based on CCP 1161a, a requirement that is only applicable to residential properties per CCP 1166(c);on the other hand, on the Civil Case Cover sheet Plaintiff marks this cases as a commercial unlawful detainer. This discrepancy materially affects the rights of the defendant re: CCP 1161a, which is not applicable to commercial unlawful detainers, but is applicable to residential unlawful detainers per CCP 1161a. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 12, 2024 line 11. DEFENDANT ALAN FORBES DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR. First Amended Complaint on file. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
